description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.

A revitalized bow, reloaded as part of an Attack Action.

+1 to Attack.
A sight midification for any ranged weapon. Provides +1 to hit against targets within 12m.
Increase Body by 2 (and related stats). Stacks. Max at 10 Body.

No immediate damage, no ablation. Inflicts Sleep status. DV13 Resist Torture/Drugs Check (Physical for most mooks);on fail falls Prone (see pg. 169) and unconscious for 1 minute.. Lasts for 10 triggers/10 rounds/5 minutes/until an antidote is administered. New application refreshes the timer. Once the target is asleep, they no longer attempt the checks and remain unconscious for 1 minute in combat, 1 hour out of combat, until non-status-effect damage is taken, or until they're woken up as an action. Once woken, if enough time hasn't passed, they may be at risk of falling back asleep!

No immediate damage, no ablation. Inflicts Poison status. DV13 Resist Torture/Drugs Check (Physical for most mooks); on a fail 2d6 damage to HP. Lasts for 10 triggers/10 rounds/5 minutes/until an antidote is administered. New application refreshes the timer. Stacks with Biotoxin. Does not cause a sleeping target to wake.

Netrunner armored skinsuit. Can store Cyberdeck and support Interface Plugs. Can't be bought in two pieces. Always worn on both Head and Body Location. Adds one Hardware only Option Slot to connected Cyberdeck. No armor penalty.

Designed to interface with the profile and features of a bodyweight suit, the active cloaking device camoflages its user for 30 seconds after being activated as an action. They gain +4 to stealth checks, and attacks or checks to see them are made with a cumulative -4 penalty. Recharges 12 hours after use.
Two Handed Exotic Very Heavy Melee Weapon. When wielded by the correct user the weapon ignores armor less than 7 SP, full details on page 92.

information.notes:
WARNING: These mooks are not balanced for general play. Each of them is a somehow-busted combination of stuff included within the mook pack that was discovered during testing. Turnabout is fair play. Use at your own risk!!

Gang Wide Ability: Night City Nightmare - Nightmares are designed to be elite, boss+ tier enemies, and defeating them yields special rewards, as follows:

  • The player performing the coup de grace immediately gains 20 IP or 1 personal rep which expires after 1 in-game week.

    • The player dealing the final blow may choose up to 2 assistants - the assistants are awarded up to 10 IP each at GM discretion.

  • Scavenging the corpse of the enemy always yields 1 single-use powerup - the enemy type determines the powerup, which is listed as part of its ability. It also always yields 100eb of scrap.

Nightmares don't go down easily - by the time one is killed, its gear is rendered unusable.

Ability - From The Shadows: When a Nightmare Stalker rolls an attack, if they roll that attack from stealth, that attack always deals at least half its potential damage. If the attack inflicts a critical injury, the stalker can choose the critical injury instead of rolling for it. When killed a Stalker drops 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsItems22.axLPuSaukO0KlQi2]{Stalker Stealth}

Ability - He's In The Walls…: Once a turn, a Stalker can pass through one wall as though it wasn't there. A Nightmare Stalker can dodge bullets, and uses the physical skill for purposes of doing so.

Stalkers are the assassins of Night City, taking pages straight out of Arasaka's playbook for doing so. They likely don't have the same level of intel, but they'll give any high-end elite a run for their money, and get out before they're ever seen.
